author: PC Gamer
text:
Everything we know about Silent Hill: Townfall and its foggy Scottish town
Townfall's got medical trauma, fog, and a radio. Sounds like Silent Hill to me.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Microsoft is looking to speed up development of future Elder Scrolls, Fallout, and Halo games
A new report suggests Microsoft may also be giving thought to spinning off its Xbox division or selling it entirely.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Tracing blueprints in Paralives is so easy it freed me from 25 years of The Sims' grid-based tyranny
One tiny mod for Paralives has finally made my childhood dreams of more realistic life sims floor plans possible.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Even a fantasy game like Skyrim won't let me be a trillionaire
I can be a werewolf or a vampire, sure, but nothing as far-fetched as a trillionaire.

author: PC Gamer
text:
RPG Maker forums are closing, nearly 15 years of knowledge and online culture are at risk of being wiped
Gotcha Gotcha Games is launching a new forum, which is good, but says it won't archive the old one, which is very bad.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 11 is one of the greatest MMOs of our time
Revisiting an enduring MMORPG.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Over the Hill's Next Fest demo promises a stylized off-roading game for people who actually like to drive
Forza Horizon is fine, I guess, but for my money this is where it's at.

author: PC Gamer
text:
ProtoArc EM25 ergonomic mouse review
Rough around the edges.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Consumer rights champion and tech-whizz Louis Rossman is taking Samsung to court over a failed 990 Pro SSD it says it can't replace, even though Amazon has plenty of them in stock
The fact that they now cost $949 instead of $300 surely has nothing to do with it, right?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Former Destiny 2 CM claims Bungie was 'very close to shutting its doors' before the buyout
"It was an emergency acquisition."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dutch non-profit set to take Valve to court for keeping game prices high
Someone else has a bone to pick with Valve's commission on Steam

author: PC Gamer
text:
Judging by how well Gothic Remake has sold, we still love eurojank
The remake of the creaky 25-year-old RPG has already hit 500k sales.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I'm sad, I'm angry, I'm interested: Destiny's final cutscenes are a painful end to a new saga cut short
Finish the fight.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Possibly the first instance of Asus' anti-melting 12V-2×6 power cable…err…melting shows up, adding more fuel to the fire that is Nvidia's connector
Normally I'd suggest burning such things to the ground but they're doing that themselves.

author: PC Gamer
text:
OpenAI says it busted a shadowy Chinese operation that used ChatGPT to whip up data centre hate (and that achieved basically nothing)
Hey, it's a living.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Havn is making a smaller, lighter version of the excellent HS 420, with a few convenient upgrades too
The HS 360 is smaller and a little more convenient compared to the HS 420.

author: PC Gamer
text:
17 years ago, one engineer made a very important discovery: If you shout at data centre HDDs, they slow down
Same, little HDD. Same.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Arm's ray-traced demo of its neural technologies for mobile phone gaming proves that there's an alternate chip for handheld gaming PCs to use outside of AMD or Intel
After all, the hardware inside your phone is rather wasted, so let's stick it in something better.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The 9 games we're most excited about after Summer Game Fest's showcase bonanza
There's a lotta games to be hyped for, but these have our utmost attention.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've beat Ascension 10 with every character in Slay the Spire 2, and these are the Silent cards I recommend
How to climb the Ascensions as Slay the Spire's silent rogue.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Steam Frame is arriving in the US, suggesting the VR headset's launch isn't far off
Release may be soon, but pricing still remains a mystery.

author: PC Gamer
text:
If I had known how incredibly useful a NAS would be, I would've set one up a lot sooner
I'm running a NAS and loving it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I watched the new Halo remake gameplay, then replayed the original to nitpick the differences
Different, but not necessarily better.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Why do mech games rarely let you leave the cockpit? Brigador Killers devs joke that the feature 'added five years of development time'
10 years after the first game, Brigador Killers is mixing things up.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After playing the new remake of Tomb Raider, I'm convinced you can't actually remake Tomb Raider
The 1996 game's legacy overshadows what actually made it so distinct 30 years ago.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Time to get a little sadder about Destiny 2: TWIDs are coming to an end
No great surprise, given the end of Destiny 2 update, but seeing seeing the words put to paper is a bummer.

author: PC Gamer
text:
You can just bypass Fable's reputation system with enough gold, which seems like a waste of its 'complex, nuanced, take on morality'
Fable's take on morality sounds pretty neat—if only it weren't so eager to assure us we can just buy out of it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A single crab has the third highest player kill count in Path of Exile 2
Grinding Gear Games briefly created one of the most powerful monsters in the action RPG—on accident.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Quake Champions gets a huge update and free battle pass to celebrate the 30th anniversary of Quake
No, it is not dead, and if you've ever wanted to jump in, this would be a great time.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Pokémon Go data was used to help train AI systems being developed for military drones
Niantic Spatial says "ground scans" collected through Pokémon Go are part of the data being used to train its models.
